summary: Decompiled/high-matched the Snap menu per-frame update `fn_802545C4`, a large `mnSnap_804A0A10.state` switch covering slot selection, photo browsing, dialogs, and copy/move/delete/printer/view operations. The PR body reports 99.373% match, while the decomp-dev bot slice reports GALE01 `main/melee/mn/mnsnap` `fn_802545C4` improving from 80.82% to 98.94%. It also corrected the `mnSnap_8025409C` API from a fake `HSD_JObj*` flag to `s32 dlg_type`. Review pushed back on assert/string SDATA hacks; the author reverted them as unnecessary.

key_files.role: Main decomp/matching work for `fn_802545C4`; added `mnSnap_InitDialogText`, used `PAD_STACK(336)`, direct `mnSnap_804A0A10` accesses, scoped temporaries, `#pragma dont_inline` around `mnSnap_80254014`, and updated `mnSnap_8025409C` callers to integer dialog types.
Header prototype fix: `mnSnap_8025409C(HSD_JObj* jobj_flag)` became `mnSnap_8025409C(s32 dlg_type)`.

what_changed: Reworked `fn_802545C4` into a high-match C implementation of the Snap menu's main per-frame state machine, with cases visible for states 0, 2-6, 10-17, and 20-23.

Handled Snap menu flows described in the PR body: slot/card selection, photo browsing, dialog confirmations, and card/photo operations including copy, move, delete/erase, printer, and full-view behavior.
Changed `fn_802545C4` input button storage to `u64 buttons` and assigns `mn_804A04F0.buttons = mn_80229624(4)`.
Replaced much of the local `mnSnap_State* snap` usage in `fn_802545C4` with direct `mnSnap_804A0A10` global accesses, likely for register/codegen matching.
Added `PAD_STACK(336)` and many short-lived scoped locals such as `cs0`, `cs`, `other_slot`, `thumb_img`, `next_state`, and `short card_status` to shape generated code.

Introduced `static inline void mnSnap_InitDialogText(void)` to perform repeated dialog text setup: freeing an existing `dlg_text`, allocating with `HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC`, and setting alignment, kerning, and 0.03 font size.
Marked `mnSnap_80254014` with `#pragma dont_inline on` / `#pragma dont_inline reset`.
Changed `mnSnap_8025409C` from a pointer-like `HSD_JObj* jobj_flag` parameter to `s32 dlg_type`; callers now pass `0`, `1`, or `2` instead of casts like `(HSD_JObj*) 1`.
Kept format strings such as `"%03d"` and `"%d"` as inline literals in the final diff rather than introducing named SDATA symbols.

Final header diff only visibly changes `mnSnap_8025409C`; the PR body also mentions a `mnSnap_8025441C` return-type fix, but this slice's final header hunk already shows `s32 mnSnap_8025441C(u64 buttons)` with no visible change.
smart_moves: Correcting `mnSnap_8025409C` to `s32 dlg_type` removed misleading pointer casts and made the dialog selector's real type clearer without losing matching progress.
Using a `static inline` helper for dialog text initialization reduced repeated SisLib setup while preserving inline expansion opportunities needed for matching.
Direct global-state access to `mnSnap_804A0A10` and carefully scoped locals are useful MWCC matching levers for very large state machines where a stable `snap` pointer may allocate differently.
`PAD_STACK(336)` explicitly documents a stack-padding requirement instead of hiding it in unrelated logic.
`#pragma dont_inline` around `mnSnap_80254014` is a targeted codegen control for a helper that must remain a call boundary.
After review, the PR avoided hand-expanded assert/string data hacks and returned to project-style `HSD_ASSERT`/literal-string usage where appropriate.
decomp_lessons: Do not introduce named SDATA strings for asserts or format strings just to chase matching unless the data evidence is strong; reviewer feedback here said to use `HSD_ASSERT` with implicit strings and treat format strings as inline constants.
If a parameter is semantically an integer selector, fix the type and header rather than preserving a fake pointer type for casts; this PR's `dlg_type` change is the reusable example.
Large menu state machines may match better with direct global struct references, narrow/scoped temporaries, and block-local variables, even if the result is less idiomatic C.
Repeated UI setup can be factored into a `static inline` helper when the helper is intentionally inlined and matches callsite codegen.
Use `#pragma dont_inline` sparingly and locally around the specific helper that must not inline.
Review comments can invalidate PR-body claims; compare the final diff before recording claims such as added SDATA declarations or prototype fixes.
naming_conventions: `mnSnap_8025409C(s32 dlg_type)` is preferred over `mnSnap_8025409C(HSD_JObj* jobj_flag)` because values `0`, `1`, and `2` are dialog-type selectors, not JObj pointers.
`mnSnap_InitDialogText` is a descriptive local helper name used alongside address-based symbols in `mnsnap.c`.
`fn_802545C4` remained address-named; the evidence only supports describing it as the Snap menu main per-frame update, not assigning a final semantic symbol name.

Field names exposed in this work include `active_slot`, `photo_count`, `card_status`, `dlg_active`, `dlg_timer`, `dlg_text`, `btn_idx`, `cursor_idx`, `cur_page`, `pending_loads`, `move_idx`, and `menu_sel`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Direct references to `mnSnap_804A0A10` instead of caching `mnSnap_State* snap` inside `fn_802545C4`.
Scoped locals and narrowed types to influence register allocation, including `s32 cs0`, `s32 cs`, block-local `other_slot`, `void* thumb_img`, block-local `next_state`, and `short card_status`.
Some nested menu `switch` logic was reshaped into `if`/`else if` chains, consistent with codegen-matching pressure in the diff.
Retained inline constants for SisLib format strings (`"%03d"`, `"%d"`) instead of forcing SDATA symbols.
Review-endorsed tactic: prefer project macros such as `HSD_ASSERT` over hand-expanded `__assert` blocks with custom string symbols unless the original code requires explicit expansion.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.feedback: Asked to revert hand-expanded assert blocks and use `HSD_ASSERT` with implicit strings.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.feedback: Repeated the same concern for a second assert block in thumbnail/blank-image handling.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.feedback: Said format strings likely were inline constants, not variables that had been inlined.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned the need for added SDATA string declarations such as `"jobj"`, `"jobj.h"`, `"%03d"`, and `"%d"`.

review_feedback.author: malvarezcastillo
review_feedback.feedback: Author replied that the hack was reverted as unnecessary.

follow_up_queries: Find remaining `mnsnap` functions or headers where integer selectors are typed as pointers or cast through `HSD_JObj*`.
Compare the final `fn_802545C4` match after merge against the PR title/body and the decomp-dev bot's 98.94% report to identify the remaining mismatch.
Audit whether `mnSnap_8025441C` had a return-type fix in an earlier commit; the supplied final header diff does not show it.
Search other menu files for repeated `HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC` dialog text initialization that could reuse the same static-inline matching pattern.
Search for manually declared assert or format-string SDATA symbols in menu decomp work and verify whether they should be reverted to `HSD_ASSERT` or inline literals.
